subject: Mathematics and Statistics
note: Achievement objectives from the New Zealand Curriculum. Verify against the Ministry of Education's Tāhūrangi platform (newzealandcurriculum.tahurangi.education.govt.nz) for the latest refreshed content.

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Use a range of counting, grouping, and equal-sharing strategies with whole numbers and fractions

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Know the forward and backward counting sequences of whole numbers to 100

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Know groupings with five, within ten, and with ten

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Communicate and explain counting, grouping, and equal-sharing strategies using words, numbers, and pictures

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Generalise that the next counting number gives the result of adding one object to a set and that counting the number of objects in a set tells how many

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Create and continue sequential patterns

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Order and compare objects or events by length, area, volume and capacity, weight (mass), turn (angle), temperature, and time by direct comparison and/or counting whole numbers of units

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Sort objects by their appearance

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Give and follow instructions for movement that involve distances, directions, and half or quarter turns

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Describe their position relative to a person or object

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Conduct investigations using the statistical enquiry cycle: gathering, sorting and counting, and displaying category data; discussing the results

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Use simple additive strategies with whole numbers and fractions

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Know forward and backward counting sequences with whole numbers to at least 1000

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Know the basic addition and subtraction facts

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Know how many ones, tens, and hundreds are in whole numbers to at least 1000

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Know simple fractions in everyday use

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Investigate and describe in words, rules for continuing number and spatial patterns

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Describe the characteristics of two- and three-dimensional objects using the language of geometry

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Partition and/or combine like measures and communicate findings, using numbers, words, and pictures

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Conduct investigations using the statistical enquiry cycle: gathering, sorting, and displaying category and whole-number data; communicating findings based on the data

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Compare statements with the features of simple data displays from statistical investigations or probability activities undertaken by others

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Use a range of additive and simple multiplicative strategies with whole numbers, fractions, decimals, and percentages

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Know basic multiplication and division facts

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Know how many tenths, hundredths, and thousandths are in decimals

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Know fractions and percentages in everyday use

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Record and interpret additive and simple multiplicative strategies, using words, diagrams, and symbols with understanding

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Describe and continue patterns with fractions, decimals, and integers, and represent these using graphs, tables, and diagrams

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Use linear scales and whole numbers of metric units for length, area, volume and capacity, weight (mass), angle, temperature, and time

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Find areas of rectangles and volumes of cuboids by applying multiplication

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Classify plane shapes and prisms by their spatial features

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Use a co-ordinate system or the language of direction and distance to specify locations and describe paths

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Describe the transformations (reflection, rotation, translation, or enlargement) that have mapped one object onto another

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Conduct investigations using the statistical enquiry cycle: gathering, sorting, and displaying multivariate category and whole-number data and simple time-series data to answer questions; identifying patterns and trends in context, within and between data sets

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Order the likeliness of outcomes for situations involving chance, using everyday language and simple fractions

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Use a range of multiplicative strategies when operating on whole numbers

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Understand addition and subtraction of fractions, decimals, and integers

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Find fractions, decimals, and percentages of amounts expressed as whole numbers, simple fractions, and decimals

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Apply simple linear proportions, including ordering fractions

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Know the equivalent decimal and percentage forms for everyday fractions

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Know the relative size and place value structure of positive and negative integers and decimals to three places

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Form and solve simple linear equations

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Relate tables, graphs, and equations to linear and simple quadratic relationships found in number and spatial patterns

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Use appropriate scales, devices, and metric units for length, area, volume and capacity, weight (mass), temperature, angle, and time

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Use side or edge lengths to find the perimeters and areas of rectangles, parallelograms, and triangles and the volumes of cuboids

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Deduce the angle properties of intersecting and parallel lines and the angle properties of polygons and apply these properties

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Plan and conduct investigations using the statistical enquiry cycle: determining appropriate variables and data collection methods; comparing distributions visually; communicating findings using appropriate displays

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Evaluate statements made by others about the findings of statistical investigations and probability activities

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Investigate situations that involve elements of chance by comparing experimental distributions with expectations from models of the possible outcomes, acknowledging variation and independence

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Reason with linear proportions

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Use prime numbers, common factors and multiples, and powers (including square roots)

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Understand operations on fractions, decimals, percentages, and integers

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Use rates and ratios

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Know and apply standard form, significant figures, rounding, and decimal place value

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Form and solve linear and simple quadratic equations

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Relate graphs, tables, and equations to linear, quadratic, and simple exponential relationships found in number and spatial patterns

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Apply the properties of arithmetic and geometric sequences and series

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Deduce and use formulae to find the perimeters and areas of polygons and the volumes of prisms

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Find the perimeters and areas of circles and composite shapes and the volumes of prisms, including cylinders

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Use trigonometric ratios and the theorem of Pythagoras in two dimensions

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Apply deductive reasoning to interpret and justify results in geometric contexts

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Plan and conduct surveys and experiments using the statistical enquiry cycle; evaluate the quality of data and the trustworthiness of conclusions

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Compare and describe the variation between theoretical and experimental distributions in situations that involve elements of chance

objectives.subject: Mathematics
objectives.text: Calculate probabilities, using fractions, percentages, and ratios
